name: Puig de Galatzó Loop Trail
name_original: Circular clásica al Puig de Galatzó — Paraje Natural de la Serra de Tramuntana
description: Hike the moderate 6.5-mile (10.5 km) Classic circular route to Puig de Galatzó for panoramic views in Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park.
region_intro: Hiking around Estellencs offers diverse trails within the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, a UNESCO World Heritage site characterized by its rocky coastline, steep cliffs, and terraced landscapes. The region features ancient stone terraces and winding paths through pine forests, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Hikers can explore routes ranging from coastal paths to mountain ascents, all set against the backdrop of the Mediterranean Sea. Best hiking trails around Estellencs The most popular hiking route is Puig de Galatzó Loop Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Estellencs Cove – Trail to Cala Estellencs loop from Estellencs, an easy 2.2 miles (3.6 km) path. This trail descends through agricultural terraces to a secluded pebble cove. Local hikers also love the View of Estellencs – Mirador de Ricard Roca loop from es Grau, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Estellencs is defined by its Serra de Tramuntana mountains, rocky coastline, and ancient terraced landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Estellencs are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 5,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Estellencs's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Estellencs?
A: Estellencs offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking routes. These trails vary in difficulty, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, all set within the stunning Serra de Tramuntana landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Estellencs?
A: Yes, Estellencs has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estellencs Cove – Trail to Cala Estellencs loop from Estellencs&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.2-mile (3.6 km) path descends through ancient agricultural terraces to the secluded Cala Estellencs, offering beautiful views and a tranquil spot by the sea.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Estellencs?
A: Hiking in Estellencs offers a rich variety of landscapes. You'll encounter the majestic mountains and steep cliffs of the Serra de Tramuntana, ancient stone terraces built by the Moors, winding paths through pine forests, and breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea. The descent to Cala Estellencs, for example, winds through terraces of olive, almond, and citrus trees.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Estellencs?
A: Yes, many routes around Estellencs are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000679855/puig-de-galatzo-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Galatzó Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11974549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Estellencs – Mirador de Ricard Roca loop from es Grau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several natural and historical attractions. The secluded Cala Estellencs is known for its crystal-clear waters and a small waterfall of fresh mountain spring water. You can also explore sections of the historic GR221 (Dry Stone Route) with its impressive alpine landscape and dry stone walls. For panoramic views, consider a hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/140523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Galatzó Summit&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3386326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tramuntana Mountains and Mallorca's west coast&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trails in Estellencs?
A: Estellencs is accessible by public transport, though services might be less frequent than in larger towns. It's advisable to check local bus schedules (TIB) for routes connecting to Estellencs, especially if you plan to hike sections of the GR221 that might not be circular.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Estellencs?
A: Parking is generally available within or near the village of Estellencs. Specific parking areas for trailheads may vary, so it's recommended to check local signage upon arrival. For popular routes, arriving earlier in the day can help secure a spot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Estellencs?
A: The hiking experience in Estellencs is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,200 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning coastal and mountain views, the tranquility away from mass tourism, and the diverse terrain that caters to various fitness levels. The charm of the village itself is also a highlight after a day on the trails.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or viewpoints to visit in the area?
A: Absolutely. The 16th-century Torre del Verger is a medieval watchtower offering impressive panoramic views of the coastline and the village, perfect for photography. The village of Estellencs itself, with its traditional stone houses and narrow cobbled streets, is a charming historical attraction. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/338149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sa Dragonera from Puig de les Basses&lt;/a&gt; along some trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Estellencs?
A: The best time for hiking in Estellencs is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant with flora. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers cooler temperatures but some trails might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any hikes that lead to a beach or cove?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estellencs Cove – Trail to Cala Estellencs loop from Estellencs&lt;/a&gt; is a perfect example. This easy trail takes you directly down to Cala Estellencs, a secluded pebble and rock cove with crystal-clear waters, ideal for a refreshing swim after your walk.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the entire Serra de Tramuntana mountain range?
A: Many trails in Estellencs provide expansive views of the Serra de Tramuntana. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/140523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Galatzó Summit&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers panoramic vistas over a significant portion of Mallorca, including the majestic mountain range and the coastline.
tips:
At Pas da na Sabatera, the Shoemaker's Pass, a narrow path leads off the main trail to Puig de Galatzó.

Under a rock overhang that offers good shelter even in heavy rain (as long as the wind isn't blowing from the wrong direction), there's a very simple bench where you can rest before continuing the climb.
Great hike and the view of the mountain on one side, sea on the other side - perfect!
Space to rest with a wonderful view, sure-footedness and fun climbing are a prerequisite

Vantage point with a view over the forest, the Ma-10 to the sea, a rock massif to the left. Just a short detour from the main trail.
No seating.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Classic circular route to Puig de Galatzó?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate to hard, requiring good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy hiking shoes. While komoot rates it as moderate, the terrain includes steep, rocky paths and some scrambling sections, especially as you approach the summit. It's a rewarding challenge for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: Where does the circular route to Puig de Galatzó typically start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The classic circular route has several starting points. Popular options include routes from Estellencs or Puigpunyent. Parking availability can vary, so it's advisable to research specific trailhead parking before you go. Some routes, like the one from Estellencs, are well-regarded for their clear signage and circular nature.

faqs.question: Is the Classic circular route to Puig de Galatzó dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park generally allows dogs, specific rules regarding leashes and access to certain areas may apply. Given the challenging terrain, including steep, rocky sections and scrambling, this particular route might be difficult for some dogs. Always ensure your dog is capable of handling the conditions and check local regulations before heading out.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Puig de Galatzó route is typically during the spring (April-May) or autumn (September-November) when temperatures are milder. In autumn, you might also enjoy the sight of red-orange madroños. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions or even snow at higher elevations, making the trail more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the public trails within the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. However, always respect private property and follow any posted signs regarding access or regulations. The park aims to preserve its natural and cultural heritage, so please stick to marked paths and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain, starting with wooded slopes, often through holm oak forests. As you ascend, the landscape transitions to steep, rocky sections with some scrambling. Along the way, you might spot ancient dry-stone constructions. The scenery is spectacular, culminating in magnificent 360-degree panoramic views from the summit.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable points. You'll pass through the Na Sabatera Pass and enjoy multiple views of Puig de Galatzó before reaching the Puig de Galatzó Summit itself. From the summit, you can see the Bay of Palma, the central plain, and on clear days, even Ibiza. Another great spot is the Pla de ses Serveres Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the GR 221 Dry Stone Route: Stage 2: Coma d'en Vidal-Esporles, as well as the camí de Ses Serveres, pas des Cossis, camí de sa coma d'en Vidal (GR-221), Camí de sa Coma des Moro, Camí de Son Nicolau, and Camí des Coll des Carniceret.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Tramuntana is home to characteristic fauna. While hiking, you might spot hedgehogs, weasels, and wild goats. The area is also rich in bird species, making it a great spot for birdwatching. Remember to observe wildlife from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Yes, the villages often used as starting points, such as Puigpunyent and Estellencs, offer amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options in these charming Mallorcan towns, perfect for a pre-hike coffee or a post-hike meal and rest.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as moderate to hard, with steep, rocky sections and scrambling, this particular circular route to Puig de Galatzó is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness, making it more suitable for experienced hikers.
